name: Advanced Oxygen Generation Assembly (AOGA)
short_description: ESDMD/MCO upgrade of the ISS OGA — separate H2 and RSA domes (OHDA), nitrogen-purge dormancy capability, updated cell-stack materials. AOGA mod kit launches to ISS fall 2026 to permanently replace legacy OGA.
function_role: Improved electrolytic O2 production from water, lower power per kg O2 than baseline OGA.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 157887 — Advanced Oxygen Generation Assembly (AOGA)

techport_metadata.novelty: Maintainable component-level pressure dome design; nitrogen-purge dormancy for Mars transit; flush capability removes recirculation-loop contaminants

name: High Pressure Electrochemical Oxygen Generation
short_description: Giner Inc. SBIR — high-pressure (0–3600 psi) electrochemical O2 concentrator integrating low-pressure static-vapor-feed electrolyzer in shared-end-plate stack. Avoids combustible-mixture risk of standard high-differential electrolyzers.
function_role: O2 generation at storage-tank pressure, simpler ECLSS architecture.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 90181 — High Pressure Electrochemical Oxygen Generation

inputs.conditions: higher than OGA due to pressure

techport_metadata.novelty: High-pressure O2 directly from electrolyzer (no separate compressor); cathode O2 consumption avoids gas-mixture safety risk; shared-end-plate stack integration
techport_metadata.phase_I_throughput: 0.9 kg O2/day end of Phase I

name: FY18-C1 GRC: H2O Electrolysis with Impure Water Source
short_description: GRC IRAD project to design a water electrolyzer that uses contaminated water directly, eliminating the upstream purification subsystem typically required by H2/O2 electrolyzers.
function_role: O2/H2 production directly from greywater or partially-treated ISRU water; reduces water polishing demand.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 95135 — H2O Electrolysis with Impure Water Source (GRC)

techport_metadata.responsible_program: Center Independent Research & Development: GRC IRAD
techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX07 / 07.1 ISRU / 07.1.3 Resource Processing for Production of Mission Consumables
techport_metadata.novelty: Eliminates upstream water-purification subsystem by tolerating ionic contaminants on catalyst/membrane

name: Molten Regolith Electrolysis Tech Maturation (MRE)
short_description: Lunar Resources Inc. project to mature molten regolith electrolysis — melts regolith and passes current through the melt to separate metals (iron, silicon, aluminum) from oxygen at electrodes. Joule heating sustains melt temperature.
function_role: ISRU O2 production from regolith without water — also yields construction-relevant metals.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 116413 — Molten Regolith Electrolysis Tech Maturation (MRE)

trl.source_ref: TechPort 116413 — Current TRL TBD per page

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX07 / 07.1 ISRU / 07.1.3 Resource Processing for Production of Mission Consumables
techport_metadata.novelty: Single-step extraction of metals AND O2 from any lunar mineral composition with in-situ power; metals separated by breakdown voltage; supports Artemis lunar surface demo

name: A Solar Powered, Ceramic Oxygen Concentrator
short_description: JSC CIF project — solid-state ceramic oxygen concentrator using monolithic-wafer electrolytic transfer; produces high-purity (>99.9%) high-pressure (200 psig) O2 without mechanical compressor. PRIMARILY a terrestrial pneumonia-treatment device.
function_role: Low-mass low-maintenance O2 enrichment from low-O2 source streams (e.g. impure ISRU output).

sources.title: NASA TechPort 12428 — Solar-Powered Ceramic Oxygen Concentrator

techport_metadata.novelty: Solid-state monolithic wafer (no moving parts); proven 14-month uptime in Afghanistan field hospital; <12 units in existence at scrape time. Primary intent is global health, space is secondary
techport_metadata.context_warning: This project's primary motivation is childhood pneumonia treatment in developing countries, not space O2 supply. Demoted relevance to Mars architecture.

name: ISRU-ISRU Hydrogen Oxygen Production (IHOP-BAA)
short_description: GCD project to integrate water purification (Ionomer-membrane Water Processor — IWP) and PEM water electrolysis for lunar regolith water. Goal: 1.34 kg/hr H2O purification + 0.14 kg/hr H2 + 1.16 kg/hr O2 from purified water.
function_role: Joint H2 + O2 ISRU plant — couples to Sabatier and provides propellant + life support.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 116390 — ISRU Hydrogen Oxygen Production (IHOP-BAA)

trl.source_ref: TechPort 116390 — IWP reached TRL 5, electrolyzer only TRL 4 (long-duration test not achieved)

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX07 / 07.1 ISRU / 07.1.3 — cross-cuts TX01 Propulsion, TX03 Power Storage, TX06 Health/LSS, TX07 Exploration Destination
techport_metadata.novelty: Integrated water-purification + electrolysis subsystem for lunar/Martian contaminated water; 2-yr lunar-environment design with freeze/thaw survival; leverages Boeing CST-100 Paragon water-clean bundle heritage
techport_metadata.honest_outcome: TRL 5 IWP achieved; TRL 5 electrolyzer NOT achieved due to technical difficulties testing the IWP in long-duration integrated mode

name: Mars Food Refrigeration
short_description: ESDMD/MCO project (JSC lead) to develop cold-storage system tailored for Mars surface that minimizes mass/volume while extending food shelf life to 5 years. Identifies most favorable cooling tech for the unique low-gravity, mass-constrained environment.
function_role: Refrigerated storage of crops, processed foods, and cultured products to maintain nutrient quality.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.3 Human Health and Performance / 06.3.5 Food Production, Processing, and Preservation
techport_metadata.novelty: 5-year shelf-life target for refrigerated/frozen food; cooling tech selection for low-g operation; decision-driver for ECLSS food architecture

name: ERASMUS: Food Contact Safe Plastics Recycler and 3D Printer System
short_description: Tethers Unlimited SBIR Phase II — integrates plastics recycler + dry-heat sterilizer + 3D printer to convert spent plastic packaging into food-grade and medical-grade printer filament for utensils/implements/medical devices.
function_role: Closes the food packaging waste loop — converts pouches into reusable utensils/containers.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 93470 — ERASMUS: Food Contact Safe Plastics Recycler and 3D Printer

techport_metadata.novelty: Integrated steriliz-recycle-print pipeline from packaging waste to food-contact-safe utensils/medical devices; closes packaging waste loop

name: A Multipurpose Fruit and Vegetable Processing System for Advanced Life Support (MFVP)
short_description: JSC HRP project (UC Davis collaborator) — miniaturized integrated fruit/vegetable processor with optimized membranes for tomato-juice concentration and electrolyzed-water cleaning-in-place (CIP). Used ESM-driven design throughout.
function_role: Bridge from raw harvested crops to crew-ready ingredients with minimal crew time.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 23598 — Multipurpose Fruit and Vegetable Processing System

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.3 Human Health and Performance / 06.3.5 Food Production, Processing, and Preservation
techport_metadata.novelty: First integrated miniaturized fruit/vegetable unit operations chain (wash, peel, slice, juice, concentrate); ESM-driven design as primary management tool; demonstrated electrolyzed-water CIP as detergent/disinfectant-free cleaning

name: Food Water Reduction
short_description: ESDMD/MCO project (JSC lead) — reduce food-system water content from current ~45% to ~30% by reformulating/processing nearly half of thermostabilized foods as freeze-dried, then evaluating shelf life. Couples directly to ECLSS oxygen-recovery efficiency.
function_role: Recovers ~80% of food waste water content for return to potable polish; reduces waste mass.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.3 Human Health and Performance / 06.3.5 Food Production, Processing, and Preservation (cross-cuts 06.1 ECLSS)
techport_metadata.novelty: Direct lever on ECLSS water-recycling efficiency via food-system water-content reduction; quantifies feasibility of 45% → 30% reformulation

name: Advanced Organic Waste Gasifier (AOWG)
short_description: Pioneer Astronautics SBIR — steam-reformation + electrolysis converts organic waste (food, packaging, paper, wipes, gloves, feces, urine brine, MAGs) into clean water + small inorganic residue + ventable gases. Adds feed-prep, continuous feeder, tar-destruction reactor over baseline TtG.
function_role: Aggressive thermal conversion of organic + plastic mixed waste; alternative to torrefaction/pyrolysis.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 94479 — Advanced Organic Waste Gasifier, Phase I

techport_metadata.novelty: Tar-destruction reactor for clean water output (vs prior TtG); continuous feeder for microgravity ops; broad waste tolerance including urine brine and MAGs

name: Integrated LED/Imaging Illumination Panels for Plant Growth Chamber
short_description: SSC CIF — aeroponic plant growth chamber demonstrating multi-color RGB LED clusters with integrated imaging cameras. Replaceable LED nodes (cost reduction), DC-power safety in wet environments, two illumination modes (germination vs maturation). NOT a full crop production system.
function_role: Compact aeroponic crop production with built-in monitoring; alternative to wick/passive Veggie design.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 17352 — Aeroponic Plant Growth Chamber w/ LED + Cameras

techport_metadata.novelty: Imaging cameras integrated INTO LED illumination panels; replaceable LED node clusters (vs commercial fixed panels); low-voltage DC for wet-environment safety; spectral/intensity modulation feedback loop
techport_metadata.context_warning: Project scope is the lighting panel + chamber demo, not a full crop production architecture

name: Improving Habitability, Mood & Diet through Bioregenerative Food Systems (SmartPot/SPOT)
short_description: STRG grant at U Colorado Boulder — modified AeroGarden hydroponic platform (SmartPot/SPOT) with AgQ AI-for-agriculture software, robotic automation, and human-plant interaction studies. Researched habitat-integrated bioregenerative food vs separate growth chambers.
function_role: Automated crop production reducing crew burden via robotic harvest/maintenance.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 11493 — Improving Habitability, Mood & Diet through Bioregenerative Food Systems

techport_metadata.novelty: Human-plant interaction (HPI) psychophysiology measurement (BioPac); SPOT 4.0 system with new pH-stable electronics; deployed at MDRS Mars-160 analog mission; non-invasive crew/SPOT location tracking with 25cm accuracy

name: Mars Regolith Water Extractor (MRWE)
short_description: Pioneer Astronautics SBIR — heated CO2 stream (solar or nuclear waste heat) passed through Mars regolith to outgas absorbed water; Mars cold ambient condenses water out of CO2 sweep stream. Eliminates need to launch H2 to Mars for Sabatier propellant production.
function_role: Water extraction from hydrated minerals; competing approach to auger dryer.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 8856 — Mars Regolith Water Extractor

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX07 / 07.1 ISRU / 07.1.3 Resource Processing for Production of Mission Consumables
techport_metadata.novelty: Recirculating CO2 sweep stream uses Mars ambient cold for condensation (no active chiller); enables full Mars-to-Earth propellant manufacture via S/E cycle without zirconia or RWGS; secondary terrestrial application for water-stressed regions
techport_metadata.viking_water_basis: Viking measurements show Mars regolith ≥1% water by weight, probably >3%

name: Extraction and Utilization of Water and Gases from Icy Lunar Regolith
short_description: Faraday Technology + U Kansas SBIR — extracts water AND volatile gases (H2S, NH3, SO2, C2H4, CO2, CH3OH, CH4) from icy lunar PSR regolith using thermosensitive liquids for separation. Phase II target: 7 kg/hr semi-continuous extraction.
function_role: Bulk water + volatile gas (N2, CO, CH4) recovery from icy regolith deposits.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 154566 — Extraction and Utilization of Water and Gases from Icy Regolith

techport_metadata.novelty: Joint water + volatile-gas extraction (vs water-only); thermosensitive solvents for separation/regeneration; volatile gas products usable for polythiols polymer, batteries, sealants; Artemis + Xelene commercial alignment
techport_metadata.context_warning: Lunar PSR focus, not Mars-rated. Mars adaptation TBR.

name: CRATOS II: Modular ISRU Excavation Tools
short_description: GRC CIF — self-assembling, intelligent autonomous track mechanisms with Zigbee mesh communication. Each track recognizes peers, arbitrates communication hub, and self-organizes into excavation teams. Hardware integration of all functions inside the track.
function_role: Acquires loose and consolidated regolith feedstock for downstream water/O2 ISRU plants.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 146181 — CRATOS II Modular ISRU Excavation Tools, Year 2

outputs.units: kg/sol (regolith feedstock — not waste, going to ISRU)

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX07 / 07.1 ISRU / 07.1.2 Resource Acquisition, Isolation, and Preparation
techport_metadata.novelty: First demonstration of self-assembling autonomous excavation modules at scale relevant to NASA ISRU; graceful fleet degradation through subcomponent reorganization; embedded all functions in track hardware

name: Improvement in Space Food Packaging Methods (Bulk Overwrap Bag Phase II)
short_description: JSC IRAD — redesign of the Bulk Overwrap Bag (BOB) to fit Collapsible US Food Containers (CUFC) on-orbit and find alternative materials with O2 + water-vapor barrier sufficient for 18-month shelf life without secondary aluminum laminate overwrap.
function_role: Long-shelf-life packaging that survives Mars-class storage durations and reduces waste packaging mass.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 12423 — Improvement in Space Food Packaging Methods

techport_metadata.responsible_program: Center Independent Research & Development: JSC IRAD
techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.1 ECLSS / 06.1.2 Water Recovery and Management (note: counter-intuitively classified)
techport_metadata.novelty: Eliminates secondary aluminum-laminate overwrap to reduce mass + volume; sized for in-flight CUFC pantry geometry instead of Cargo Transfer Bags
techport_metadata.context_warning: Short 3-month scoping study, not a fully developed system. Specific to ISS Bulk Overwrap Bag baseline.

name: Torrefaction Processing for Human Solid Waste Management
short_description: Advanced Fuel Research SBIR — mild pyrolysis (200–250°C) sterilizes feces and produces stable odor-free solid plus near-complete moisture recovery. Compatible with NASA UWMS. Solid product mechanically friable for ~50% volume reduction; supports microbial-resistant storage or recycle.
function_role: Mild thermal stabilization of human waste with NASA project documentation; references TECH-0041 design space.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 89572 — Torrefaction Processing for Human Solid Waste Management

techport_metadata.novelty: Lower temperature than pyrolysis, near-complete moisture recovery, solid product is microbial-resistant + hydrophobic + friable for ~50% volume reduction; UWMS-compatible; can extend to food waste, wipes, brine. Solid residue has secondary uses (activated carbon, plant substrate, polymer filler, radiation shielding, fuel-gas precursor)

name: Fission Surface Power (FSP)
short_description: GRC-led Technology Demonstration Mission — engineering flight unit (EFU) for ≥10 kWe nuclear surface power, lunar demo extensible to Mars. Multi-vendor industry team: Aerojet Rocketdyne, BWX Tech, Creare, IX, Lockheed, Maxar, Boeing, Westinghouse + DOE.
function_role: Continuous nuclear surface power; TechPort link to NASA's official program docs.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX03 / 03.1 Power Generation / 03.1.6 Other Advanced Concepts (cross-cuts TX14 Thermal)
techport_metadata.novelty: Engineering flight unit qualified for lunar demo; ≥10 kWe class with 10-yr life; four units cluster to power Moon/Mars surface ops; multi-vendor industry maturity

name: Regenerative Fuel Cell (RFC)
short_description: GRC-led GCD project — PEM fuel cell + PEM electrolyzer for long-duration energy storage. Round-trip efficiency <60% per thermodynamics, but up to 3.4× energy storage at same mass as Li-ion. Enables lunar-night operation and PSR missions where solar+battery and nuclear are not options.
function_role: Diurnal/dust-storm energy storage; fed by solar arrays; releases stored energy at night.

supporting_organizations: Infinity Fuel Cell and Hydrogen, Inc.
techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX03 / 03.2 Energy Storage / 03.2.2 Electrochemical Storage: Fuel Cells
techport_metadata.novelty: PEM-PEM round trip; up to 3.4× energy storage vs batteries at same mass; only viable energy storage spanning lunar nights; works in extreme environments (PSR cold)

name: WILD: Waste Improved Lunar Dirt — Lunar Soil Enrichment for Plant Production
short_description: KSC IRAD project — improve fertility of lunar regolith simulant (JSC-1A) for plant growth via microbe bioleaching (Pantoea agglomerans + plant-relevant consortium) for P/K extraction PLUS composted waste addition for water retention. Identified soil-crust biofilm formation as bonus dust mitigation method.
function_role: Convert raw lunar/Martian regolith + crew waste compost into fertile soil for in-situ crop production, eliminating Earth-launched soil substrate.

sources.title: NASA TechPort 156897 — WILD: Waste Improved Lunar Dirt

techport_metadata.responsible_program: Center Independent Research & Development: KSC IRAD
techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.3 Human Health and Performance / 06.3.5 Food Production (cross-cuts TX07 / 07.1.4 ISRU Manufacturing Feedstock)
techport_metadata.novelty: Bioleaching microbes (Pantoea agglomerans) liberate P and K from regolith for plant uptake; composted human waste closes nutrient loop; bonus discovery of native JSC-1A microbes that form soil-crust biofilm (potential dust mitigation)

name: BioChar Amendments for Plant Microbiome and Crop Health
short_description: KSC IRAD project — adds biochar (pyrolyzed plant biomass) to root-module soil substrate for sequential cropping in single root module. Studies biochar effect on root-microbe interactions via metagenomic sequencing. 3D-printed root modules from APH chamber, wheat as model crop.
function_role: Improve soil fertility and microbial health for repeated croppings in single root module — reduces resupply mass for soil substrate.

techport_metadata.responsible_program: Center Independent Research & Development: KSC IRAD
techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.3 Human Health and Performance / 06.3.5 Food Production
techport_metadata.novelty: Biochar from inedible biomass closes carbon loop AND improves sequential-cropping soil health; metagenomic next-gen sequencing of root-microbe community changes; 3D-printed APH-compatible root modules

name: 3D Printed Food System for Long Duration Space Missions
short_description: SMRC SBIR Phase I — 3D-printing system delivering macronutrients (starch/protein/fat) as printed structures plus inkjet-delivered micronutrients/flavor/smell. Combines with NC State food science + International Flavors & Fragrances. Partnered with JSC.
function_role: Personalized on-demand meal production from sterile dry stocks — addresses long-shelf-life storage, variety, individual nutrition, food boredom.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.3 Human Health and Performance / 06.3.6 Long-Duration Health
techport_metadata.novelty: 3D printer + microjetting combo delivers personalized nutrition + flavor + texture from shelf-stable feedstocks; zero waste; per-crewmember dietary customization

name: Cellular Experiment Laboratory System (CELS)
short_description: Helogen Corporation Flight Opportunities project — fully-autonomous payload for in-situ cell-based experiments including organoids and organ-on-a-chip (MPS). Microfluidic + spectrophotometry + imaging without astronaut intervention or downmass.
function_role: Autonomous in-space cell-culture platform — relevant to cell-cultured meat scaling and cell-line health monitoring for cultured-protein architectures.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.3 Human Health and Performance / 06.3.1 Medical Diagnosis and Prognosis
techport_metadata.novelty: Fully-autonomous cell culture without astronaut intervention or downmass; integrates liquid handling, subculturing, spectrophotometry; modular for orbital/suborbital/station deployment
techport_metadata.context_warning: Primary application is biomedical research, not food production. Relevant as platform for cultured-meat cell-line work but not direct food tech.

name: Scaleable, High Efficiency Microchannel Sabatier Reactor (MSRS)
short_description: UMPQUA Research SBIR — array of cross-connected isothermal/graded-temperature microchannel Sabatier reactors achieves 99% single-pass CO2 conversion (vs 85% thermodynamic limit at 400°C). 1 kg/hr CO2 throughput target.
function_role: Near-complete single-pass CO2 reduction for closing the carbon-water loop with much higher efficiency than baseline Sabatier.

techport_metadata.novelty: Cross-connected microchannel reactor array with graded temperatures overcomes 85% thermodynamic limit, achieves 99% conversion; couples exothermic+endothermic reactions for self-heating efficiency

name: Novel Catalytic Reactor for CO2 Reduction via Sabatier Process (Microlith)
short_description: Precision Combustion Inc. SBIR — short-contact-time Microlith Sabatier reactor running at ≤350°C. Smaller, lighter, more durable than baseline. Methane byproduct usable as propellant or chemical feedstock.
function_role: Compact catalytic CO2-to-methane conversion for cabin atmosphere revitalization and ISRU propellant.

techport_metadata.novelty: Microlith short-contact-time geometry runs at ≤350°C (lower than typical Sabatier), more compact and durable; close-to-equilibrium conversion at high throughput; methane as propellant or chemicals feedstock

name: Vacuum-Regenerable Trace Contaminant Control (TCC) for PLSS
short_description: Precision Combustion Inc. SBIR Phase II-E — compact, low-pressure-drop, vacuum-regenerable TCC bed for spacesuit PLSS using Microlith substrate. Removes NH3 + HCHO; tolerates humid air.
function_role: Trace contaminant removal for portable life support; could be adapted for cabin TCC duty.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.2 EVA Systems / 06.2.2 Portable Life Support System
techport_metadata.novelty: Vacuum regeneration eliminates power-hungry thermal regen; humid-air tolerant (vs typical desiccant-coupled designs); compact + low-pressure-drop

name: Hybrid Photo-Catalytic Oxidation (H-PCO) for Trace Contaminant Control
short_description: KSC IRAD — annular Silica-Titania Composite (STC) UV-PCO reactor combined with sorbent for VOC removal. Operates near room temp, low-energy. Targeted at ethanol (primary VOC aboard spacecraft).
function_role: Low-power VOC removal alternative to thermal/sorbent TCC.

techport_metadata.responsible_program: Center Independent Research & Development: KSC IRAD
techport_metadata.novelty: PCO+sorbent hybrid combines low-energy room-temp operation with adsorption capacity; STC+UV-C catalyst design from KSC; specifically targets ethanol (top VOC aboard spacecraft)

name: Solid State Oxygen Concentrator and Compressor
short_description: Sustainable Innovations / Skyre Inc. SBIR — solid-state electrochemical cell separates pure O2 from cabin atmosphere. Demonstrated in mock zero-g and negative-g; can replenish secondary O2 pack (SOP) for EVA.
function_role: Compact lightweight O2 concentration alternative to pressure-swing adsorption; recycling EVA secondary O2 packs.

techport_metadata.novelty: Solid-state electrochemical cell (no moving parts); zero-g tested; can replenish EVA SOP from cabin air to extend EVA time

name: FARADAYIC Electrochemical Peroxide Generation for In-Situ Disinfection
short_description: Faraday Technology SBIR Phase IIE — in-situ generation of 0.5–6% hydrogen peroxide from onboard water + O2 for surface, laundry, and waste-water disinfection. Eliminates disposable wipe burden.
function_role: In-situ sanitizer generation closing the cleaning-supplies loop; reduces consumables and disposable mass; supports REQ-SYS-023 sanitization.

techport_metadata.novelty: Onboard electrochemical peroxide generation eliminates Earth-launched disinfectant wipes; supports reusable cloths; multi-concentration (0.5% surface vs 6% deep clean)

name: Oxygen Delivery System (Oxygen Concentrator Module)
short_description: JSC HRP — supplemental medical-O2 delivery using vacuum-swing-adsorption (VSA) on cabin air with Li-LSX zeolite. Avoids cabin O2 build-up and fire hazard from compressed-O2 delivery.
function_role: Medical / EVA pre-breathe / contingency O2 supplement; pulls O2 from cabin instead of high-pressure tanks.

techport_metadata.novelty: VSA from cabin air avoids fire-hazard pockets caused by compressed-O2 delivery; supports medical, pre-breathe, contingency, leak/contam events; subsumed into OGRe portfolio

name: MOXIE — Mars Oxygen In-Situ Resource Utilization Experiment (original)
short_description: JPL ESDMD/MCO — first ISRU tech demo on another planet. Solid oxide electrolysis (SOXE) at 800°C produces O2 from Mars CO2 atmosphere. Flew on Mars 2020 / Perseverance 2021. Target: 6 g O2/hr, 99.6% purity.
function_role: Demonstrated CO2-to-O2 conversion via SOXE; basis for scaled-up Mars surface O2 production for human missions.

techport_metadata.novelty: FIRST ISRU demo on another planet; 800°C SOXE; 50 g/hr atmosphere intake → 6 g/hr O2 at 99.6% purity; survived dust + temperature cycles on Mars surface

name: Liquid Sorption Pump (LSP) for Mars CO2 Acquisition
short_description: Pioneer Astronautics SBIR — propanol cooled below -100°C absorbs CO2 from Mars atmosphere; warmed to 30°C to release at >1 bar. Continuous day/night operation. No mechanical roughing pumps, freezers, or large sorption beds.
function_role: Mars CO2 acquisition for ISRU input — feeds Sabatier or O2 production with cleaner, more energy-efficient capture than alternatives.

techport_metadata.novelty: Continuous day/night operation; rejects sulfur, dust, non-condensables; lower power than scroll-pump or freezer alternatives; readily scalable

name: ICICLE — ISRU Collector of Ice in Cold Lunar Environment
short_description: Paragon SBIR — Cold Trap that selectively freezes water vapor from lunar PSR ice mining outputs while rejecting corrosive/toxic/explosive contaminant gases. Integrates with Honeybee Robotics Polar Volatiles Extractor (PVEx).
function_role: Water capture/purification cold trap downstream of ice mining — bridges PVEx-class extractors to electrolyzers / IHOP.

techport_metadata.novelty: Selective cold-trap freezes only water from mixed ice-mining effluent; isothermal deposition surface; integrated end-to-end with PVEx extractor in TVAC test
techport_metadata.context_warning: Lunar PSR focus — Mars adaptation TBR

name: Regolith to Steel Powder, Oxygen & Water with Small Equipment
short_description: STTR Phase I — Mars regolith → enriched iron ore + liberated water (heat-recycling) → metal reduction with H2/CO syngas → steel powder via BASF carbonyl metallurgy. Single chain produces O2, water, AND steel. Compact enough for <2500 kg lander payload.
function_role: Produces O2, water, AND construction-grade steel from Mars regolith with single integrated reactor chain — enables expanding spiral of in-situ manufacturing.

techport_metadata.novelty: Heat re-cycling between regolith enrichment + iron ore reduction liberates water as bonus; SOEC water/CO2 splitter from Ceramatec (MOXIE heritage); BASF carbonyl metallurgy for metal purification; entire chain fits <2500 kg lander payload

name: Mars Integrated Propellant Production System (IMISPPS)
short_description: Pioneer Astronautics SBIR — combines RWGS + Sabatier in a single reactor producing CH4+CO fuel + water; water electrolyzed for O2 + H2; H2 recycled to reactor. Lightweight CO2 freezer for atmosphere acquisition. Single-reactor design for Mars propellant.
function_role: End-to-end Mars propellant + life-support O2/water from Mars CO2 atmosphere using single integrated reactor.

techport_metadata.novelty: Combined RWGS+Sabatier in single reactor produces ALL propellant in one catalytic step; CO removed cryogenically to boost specific impulse; lightweight freezer CO2 acquisition

name: LuSTR — Regolith Beneficiation for Lunar ISRU
short_description: Missouri S&T — magnetic + electrostatic separation of anorthite from lunar regolith simulants. Achieved 95% purity by weight. Tested in 1/6 g parabolic flight 2025.
function_role: Pre-processes raw regolith into refined feedstock for downstream ISRU (metal/oxygen extraction, additive manufacturing, sintering). Lower SWaP-C than mechanical methods.

techport_metadata.lead_organization: Missouri University of Science and Technology
techport_metadata.novelty: Electrodynamic (no moving parts) regolith separation — magnetic + electrostatic; 95% anorthite purity; flown in lunar gravity 2025-04-29
techport_metadata.context_warning: Lunar focus — Mars adaptation TBR but principles transferable

name: Dust Mitigation Plasma Technology (DMPT)
short_description: JPL SMD — RF plasma generated from low-pressure CO2 (Mars) creates electric force to remove charged dust from solar panels, suits, visors. Studies adhesion/repulsion physics under plasma conditions.
function_role: Removes accumulated dust from external surfaces (solar arrays, ECLSS heat radiators, EVA suits, visors) — enables sustained surface ops on Mars/Moon.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX07 / 07.2 Mission Infrastructure / 07.2.5 Particulate Contamination Prevention and Mitigation
techport_metadata.novelty: Mars CO2 atmosphere directly used as plasma source via RF (no extra gas needed); active electrodynamic dust removal vs passive mitigation

name: Science Fridge/Freezer — Microgravity Vapor Compression
short_description: JSC CIF — micro-gravity-compatible vapor compression refrigerator/freezer using oil-free compressor. Stores medical/science samples at +5°C to -20°C. Replaces low-efficiency thermoelectric ISS units.
function_role: Energy-efficient cold storage compatible with low-gravity environments — applicable to food refrigeration AND science samples.

techport_metadata.novelty: Oil-free compressor enables vapor compression in micro-gravity without contamination/maintenance burden of Shuttle-era systems; more energy-efficient than ISS thermoelectric units
techport_metadata.context_warning: Short 3-month proof-of-concept; not a fully developed system

name: Multi-Environment MLI (MEMLI) — Insulation for Mars Missions
short_description: Quest Thermal Group SBIR — multi-functional thermal insulation system that performs in-air, in-space, and on-Mars surface. Supports zero-boil-off cryogenic propellant storage on Mars surface (~0.75 W/m² on Mars).
function_role: Cryogenic insulation for propellant tanks AND food cold storage AND ISRU water tanks across all mission environments.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX14 / 14.1 Cryogenic Systems / 14.1.1 In-Space Propellant Storage
techport_metadata.novelty: Single insulation system works in-air, in-space, AND on-Mars (vs separate solutions); ventable/sealable supported vacuum shell + Load Bearing MLI tuned for Mars atmosphere; ~1 kg/m² mass

name: Common Habitat for Long Duration Transit and Surface Operations
short_description: JSC IRAD — Skylab-II-derived habitat using SLS LOX tank as primary structure, with interior architecture viable for lunar surface, transit, AND Mars surface (single development for three roles). Four CAD variants for VR-based human-in-the-loop evaluation.
function_role: Pressure shell + interior architecture supporting all food production, prep, dining, ECLSS subsystems for three habitat roles.

techport_metadata.responsible_program: Center Independent Research & Development: JSC IRAD
techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX11 Modeling/Sim/IT / 11.2 Modeling / 11.2.3 Human-System Performance Modeling
techport_metadata.novelty: Single common habitat design serves lunar/transit/Mars roles (saves decade + tens of billions per separate dev); SLS LOX tank as pressure shell heritage; 15-function VR evaluation framework with Functional Analysis Matrices

name: Microbial Monitoring of Spacecraft & Habitats via Advanced Metabolomics
short_description: Connecticut Analytical SBIR — electrospray air filtration captures airborne microorganisms; mass-spec analysis identifies pathogens via metabolomic biomarkers. Also captures NASA SMAC trace species. Milliwatt power, zero pressure drop. Builds on Apollo-era heritage.
function_role: Autonomous remote pathogen + trace contaminant detection in cabin air — supports REQ-SYS-022/044 food safety monitoring during dormant Mars transit phases.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.4 Environmental Monitoring / 06.4.1 Air, Water, Microbial, and Acoustic Sensors
techport_metadata.novelty: Electrospray + mass-spec captures AND identifies pathogens autonomously; milliwatt-class power with zero pressure drop; doubles as crew-health monitor via exhaled-breath analysis

name: Space Metal Processing System (SMPS) / Modular Space Foundry
short_description: CisLunar Industries — electromagnetic-induction metal melting + manipulation in microgravity. Casts rods/tubes/sheet/wire/metal-propellant from feedstock. Free-flying contactless manipulation tested in 2024-2025 parabolic flights.
function_role: In-space metal manufacturing — could turn ISRU iron/steel from MRE/regolith-to-steel into spare parts, structures, even propellant.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX12 Materials/Manufacturing / 12.4 / 12.4.6 Recycle, Reuse, and Repurpose Processes
techport_metadata.novelty: Electromagnetic induction (vs ISS EML) enables higher throughput + continuous casting; contactless 3D molten metal manipulation (Steer); deployed as free-flyer in parabolic flight to isolate from aircraft motion

name: Non Thermal Plasma Assisted Catalytic Sabatier Reactor
short_description: Lynntech SBIR Phase II — low-temperature plasma-assisted Sabatier; eliminates the 350-400°C requirement and thermal-management exotherm headache of conventional fixed-bed Sabatier. Demonstrated 14 g/hr CH4 → scaling to 0.55 kg/hr.
function_role: Energy-efficient low-temperature CO2 methanation for cabin air rev + Mars propellant — better thermal management than conventional Sabatier.

techport_metadata.novelty: Low-temperature operation eliminates exotherm thermal-management problem of conventional Sabatier; improves catalyst durability for 18-month Mars stay

name: Universal Waste Management System (UWMS) — ISS Toilet
short_description: JSC ESDMD/MCO — universal toilet for crewed vehicles + habitats. 65% smaller, 40% lighter than ISS predecessor. Urine pretreatment dose pump enables water recovery. Already on Orion Artemis-2 and ISS demo.
function_role: Crew waste collection + urine pretreatment for downstream water recovery — feeds UPA/IHOP. Critical food-system interface (REQ-SYS-053 isolation from food prep zone).

techport_metadata.novelty: Universal across vehicles (ISS, Orion, future habitats); urine-pretreat-quality sensor and dose pump enable WRS handoff; gender-inclusive collection devices; future fecal-canister evolution targets fecal water recovery
techport_metadata.known_issues: Dose pump dispense failures, excess noise, controller function loss — vendor delivering redesigned dose pump for early 2026 launch

name: Microbiological Quality and Food Safety of Plants Grown on ISS
short_description: KSC IRAD — methods for real-time microbial sampling, rapid pathogen detection (RAZOR PCR), and sanitization (citric-acid Pro-San wipes) of crops grown on ISS Veggie/APH. 90-99.9% bacterial reduction on vegetable surfaces; 99.99% on plastic.
function_role: Validation pipeline ensuring fresh ISS-grown crops meet NASA microbiological food safety standards before crew consumption (REQ-SYS-022, REQ-SYS-044).

techport_metadata.responsible_program: Center Independent Research & Development: KSC IRAD
techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.3 Human Health and Performance / 06.3.5 Food Production
techport_metadata.novelty: 3-step approach: surface sampling (swab/tape/blender) + RAZOR PCR rapid pathogen detection (3hr vs 12-48hr conventional) + citric-acid Pro-San sanitizer wipes; demonstrated 99.9% bacterial reduction on edible surfaces

name: Deployable and Retractable Solar Array for Lunar Surface / Lander
short_description: DSS SBIR — high-spec-power retractable solar array for lunar lander mobility ops. Demonstrated multi-cycle deploy/retract/redeploy (no other solar array has done this). Replaces RTG on landers with renewable PV.
function_role: Repeatedly deployable surface power for mobile platforms (rovers, mobile habs); also relevant for protecting arrays during dust storms.

techport_metadata.novelty: Multi-cycle deploy/retract/redeploy capability (unique in space-rated arrays); high deployed strength for gravity+wind environments; modular adaptability

name: Composite Origami for Spacecraft Solar Arrays and Deployable Structures
short_description: NCSU Flight Opportunities — strain-energy deployed composite origami enables self-deploying solar arrays AND habitat structures. Stiff+soft matrix integrated hinge stores energy for self-deployment.
function_role: Compact-stowage self-deploying structure technology for both solar arrays and habitat structural elements; enables Mars-scale deployable hardware.

techport_metadata.lead_organization: North Carolina State University at Raleigh
techport_metadata.novelty: Strain-energy self-deployment (no actuators); integrated stiff+soft matrix hinge; enables ultra-compact stowage; same tech serves solar arrays + habitats

name: Modular Ultra-High Power Solar Array Architecture
short_description: DSS SBIR — modular scalable solar array from 100 kW up to multi-megawatt. >200-500 W/kg specific power, >80 kW/m³ stowage, 10x stiffer than rigid panel arrays. Targets large-scale Mars surface power.
function_role: Mars-scale primary power generation (100s kW to MWe) for sustained habitat + ISRU + manufacturing operations.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX03 / 03.1 Power Generation / 03.1.1 Photovoltaic Electrical Power
techport_metadata.novelty: Modular architecture scales 100 kW → MW class; cost savings 25-50%; specific power 200-500 W/kg; 10x deployed strength + stiffness vs rigid panels; supports LILT (low-illumination low-temp) Mars conditions

name: Simple Reliable Retractable Lunar Lander Solar Array
short_description: NeXolve SBIR — thin-film solar arrays + pneumatic deployment + passive constant-torque spring retraction. Uses LISA-T thin-film cells. Multi-cycle deploy/retract for surface mobility ops or storm protection.
function_role: Lightweight retractable surface power array; protects from dust storms by retracting; mobile-platform compatible.

techport_metadata.novelty: Pneumatic deployment + constant-torque-spring retraction (no motors/controls for retract); uses LISA-T thin-film with >300 W/kg specific power, >40 kW/m³ density

name: Microgravity Investigation for Thin-Film Hydroponics (μG-LilyPond NS-35)
short_description: Space Lab Technologies Flight Opportunities — passive-capillary thin-film hydroponics for aquatic plants (water lentils, Wolffia arrhiza). 100% edible biomass, fast-growing. Flew on Blue Origin NS-35 in 2025.
function_role: Passive water+nutrient delivery to aquatic plants in microgravity; water-lentil supplemental food source.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.3 Human Health and Performance / 06.3.5 Food Production
techport_metadata.novelty: First space tech for aquatic plants with liquid-air interface in microgravity; water lentils 100% edible (no inedible biomass), high protein; flew on Blue Origin NS-35 Sept 2025

name: μG-LilyPond — Floating Plant Pond (SBIR Phase I)
short_description: Space Lab Technologies SBIR — autonomous floating plant cultivation system for duckweed and Azolla; passive water+nutrient delivery, continuous biomass harvest, full seed-to-seed life cycle. Doubles as wastewater treatment + atmospheric revitalization.
function_role: Triple-duty system: supplemental food + CO2-to-O2 atmospheric revitalization + wastewater treatment via duckweed/Azolla cultivation.

techport_metadata.novelty: Triple-purpose: food + air rev + wastewater treatment; passive water/nutrient delivery to floating plants in microgravity; minimal crew time

name: Ohalo III — First Operational Crop Production System for Mars Transit
short_description: KSC ESDMD/MCO — prototype crop production system for Mars Transit Vehicle. Validates water delivery, volume optimization, productivity, reliability on ISS. Targets ISS launch August 2026. Designed for pick-and-eat crops.
function_role: BASELINE Mars Transit Vehicle crop production prototype — tests on ISS to inform Deep Space Transit and surface designs.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems
techport_metadata.novelty: Single growth chamber in EXPRESS rack; removable/customizable light cap; advanced water-delivery + volume-optimization concepts; prototype for actual Deep Space Transport food system; minimizes crew maintenance
techport_metadata.key_features: Seed delivery, plant-health sensors, food-safety monitoring, robotics + automation, evolvable waste collection + cleaning concepts

name: Vertical Solar Array Technology (VSAT)
short_description: LaRC GCD — autonomous deployable vertical solar arrays on masts up to 20m, designed to capture near-continuous sun at lunar South Pole. 10 kWe class. Three industry teams (Astrobotic, Honeybee, Lockheed) selected to advance for Artemis.
function_role: Surface power for high-latitude bases (lunar South Pole, Mars high latitudes) where low sun grazing angle requires elevated arrays for year-round operation.

techport_metadata.novelty: 20m mast elevates array for near-continuous sun at high-latitude (South Pole); autonomously deployable; pre-deployed before crew arrival

name: Mars Li-CO2 Batteries (Mars Air Battery, MAB)
short_description: GRC CIF — Lithium-CO2 rechargeable battery using Mars atmospheric CO2 as cathode reactant. Targets 1000 Wh/kg cell-level (~4× Li-ion) by leveraging in-situ CO2 instead of carrying cathode mass.
function_role: ISRU-coupled energy storage — uses Mars CO2 as battery reactant, dramatically reducing battery upmass for Mars surface missions.

techport_metadata.novelty: Mars atmospheric CO2 as cathode reactant — ~80% of total reactant mass comes from atmosphere; ~4× specific energy of Li-ion; multi-environment (also works in O2-rich habitat)
techport_metadata.context_warning: Mars surface conditions (-60°C, 0.6 kPa) — needs further cathode development to realize potential

name: Alkali Metal Flow Battery for Aerospace Energy Storage
short_description: JPL CIF — lithium naphthalenide solution flow battery with high-energy soluble organic/inorganic permeable membrane. Independent energy/power scaling, much higher energy density than vanadium redox.
function_role: Large-scale grid-scale energy storage for surface habitats — alternative to RFC and Li-ion for very large battery banks.

techport_metadata.novelty: Liquid lithium-naphthalenide anode + soluble redox couple in non-aqueous electrolyte; energy/power scale independently; higher energy density than vanadium redox

name: Lunar Plants (LPX) — First Plant Growth on Moon CubeSat
short_description: ARC CIF — 1U CubeSat (10cm, 0.3kg) plant habitat for first plant germination experiment on lunar surface. Tests Arabidopsis seeds under combined lunar gravity + radiation. CO2 sensor + camera tracks germination.
function_role: Validates plant biology under lunar gravity + radiation — informs scaling to surface food production.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.3 Human Health and Performance / 06.3.5 Food Production
techport_metadata.novelty: 1U CubeSat form factor (300g, 0.5L); 50 Arabidopsis seeds; passive water pump triggered after landing; CO2 trace tracks germination + photosynthesis; designed for any lander/rover to host

name: Vapor Chamber with PCM-Based Wick for Thermal Control
short_description: Advanced Cooling Technologies SBIR — vapor chamber using micro-encapsulated PCM beads as both thermal storage AND wick structure. Eliminates metal fins (50% mass reduction). 1000x effective thermal conductivity boost.
function_role: Mass-efficient thermal storage + heat exchange in one device — useful for buffering food refrigeration or ECLSS thermal loads.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX14 / 14.2 Thermal Control / 14.2.3 Heat Rejection and Storage
techport_metadata.novelty: Micro-encapsulated PCM beads serve dual role as thermal storage AND capillary wick; eliminates metal fin/foam mass; >2/3 PCM mass ratio

name: Modular In-Situ Curing Apparatus for Thermoset TPS
short_description: IOS SBIR — modular system for in-situ bonding/curing of thermoset resin to spacecraft structure. IR gel + conductive heat blankets + corona surface activation. Enables on-Mars TPS manufacturing.
function_role: Manufacturing of structural materials in-situ — could be adapted for in-habitat material/component fabrication.

techport_metadata.novelty: Eliminates need for autoclave/oven; in-line IR gel during 3D-print extrudate; modular conductive heat blankets conform to surface; corona activation for layer bonding
techport_metadata.context_warning: Primarily for thermal protection systems, low direct relevance to food. Tangential as in-situ manufacturing capability.

name: High Energy Density Lithium-Sulfur Battery
short_description: Giner SBIR — Li-S battery with 5x energy density of Li-ion (2500 Wh/kg theoretical) using novel polysulfide-shuttle-blocking coating. Targets EVA + spacecraft + future Mars rovers.
function_role: High-energy-density battery storage — could replace bulky Li-ion banks for long-duration habitats and EVA.

techport_metadata.novelty: 5x theoretical energy density vs Li-ion; novel coating blocks polysulfide-shuttle that historically limited Li-S cycle life

name: High Power Density Metal Junction Thermoelectric (TE) Generators
short_description: LaRC IRAD — metal-junction TE replaces semiconductor TE for RTG-class generators. Metals have 1000x higher electron density, enabling much higher power density and easier fabrication.
function_role: Higher-power-density alternative to MMRTG semiconductor TE for radioisotope-based or solar-supplemental power.

techport_metadata.responsible_program: Center Independent Research & Development: LaRC IRAD
techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX12 Materials / 12.1 / 12.1.6 Materials for Electrical Power Generation
techport_metadata.novelty: Metal junctions vs semiconductor — 3-4 orders of magnitude higher electron density; substantially higher power density per unit; easier fabrication (no doping)

name: In-Space Manufacturing — On Demand Manufacturing Metal (ISM-ODMM / FabLab)
short_description: MSFC GCD — Bound Metal Additive Manufacturing (BMAM) FabLab by Redwire/Techshot prints Ti-6Al-4V parts in microgravity. Met UTS, elongation, bulk density KPPs. Closed FY23 administratively but tech matures into electronics printing.
function_role: On-demand metal parts manufacturing for spares, tools, replacement components — eliminates ground-launched ORU dependency.

techport_metadata.novelty: Hybrid additive-subtractive manufacturing in microgravity; FabLab Furnace 2.25 kW sintering; printer module repurposed for On-Demand Electronics

name: ISS Additive Manufacturing Facility (AMF)
short_description: Made in Space SBIR — Additive Manufacturing Facility for ISS, demonstrates on-demand 3D printing of parts/tools/spares in microgravity. TRL 8.
function_role: On-orbit/in-habitat polymer 3D printing for parts, tools, spares — relevant for food prep utensils, structural fixtures, etc.

techport_metadata.novelty: First commercial-grade 3D printer on ISS; on-demand polymer parts; demonstrates path to broader in-space manufacturing

name: Advanced Microgravity Compatible Integrated Laundry (AMCIL)
short_description: UMPQUA SBIR — microgravity-compatible 2-phase laundry system using water jet agitation + vacuum-assisted drying + microwave dry. KC-135 microgravity-tested.
function_role: Crew clothing laundering with reduced water + power vs disposal model — closes loop on textile resupply.

techport_metadata.novelty: 2-phase liquid/liquid-vapor wash; pulsed water-jet agitation; microwave-vacuum drying + air-jet tumble; first microgravity-compatible laundry

name: Airborne Particulate Monitor for Spacecraft
short_description: Aerosol Dynamics SBIR — water-based condensation particle counter detects 10 nm to 20+ μm particles in spacecraft air. Tippable, self-sustaining; capillary-driven water transport for zero-g operation.
function_role: Continuous airborne particle monitoring for crew health + early-warning fire detection + food-prep contamination tracking.

techport_metadata.project_status: Completed (Closed Out 2020-01-30) — transitioned to Phase III
techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.4 Environmental Monitoring / 06.4.1 Air, Water, Microbial, Acoustic Sensors
techport_metadata.novelty: Capillary-recaptured water enables zero-g condensation particle counting; 10 nm sensitivity (vs typical >300 nm); long-term unattended operation

name: Advanced Gas Sensing Technology for Space Suits
short_description: IOS SBIR Phase II — fiber-optic CO2 + humidity sensor patches inside space suits. Operates in high humidity, distributed measurement. Designed for ISS EMU + xEMU compatibility.
function_role: Distributed atmosphere monitoring inside PLSS — could extend to cabin atmosphere and food-storage envelope monitoring.

techport_metadata.nasa_taxonomy: TX06 / 06.2 EVA Systems / 06.2.2 Portable Life Support System
techport_metadata.novelty: Flexible sensor patches don't disturb gas flow or human subject; distributed measurement reaches locations no other tech can; on-site calibration unit for NBL training events
